summary: The test suite comprehensively covers core PR labeling functionality (glob patterns, multiple labels, priority ordering, JSON config loading) with well-organized Pester tests. Unit tests include good error handling validation. However, rigor is weakened by: (1) fragile Act output parsing using complex regex patterns that assume specific log formatting—if act output changes, assertions fail silently; (2) missing edge cases (empty labels arrays, case sensitivity, null file lists, Windows vs. Unix path separators); (3) limited error message validation (only checks exceptions thrown, not content); (4) workflow tests rely on simple string matching rather than YAML parsing. Fixture file contents are not shown/validated. The Act-driven tests are data-driven and gated correctly, but the regex extraction of 'LABEL:' lines is error-prone. Would catch major regressions in core logic but would be brittle under workflow output format changes.
